title: WiFi Channels & Frequency Bands
description: The frequency channels used by WiFi across 2.4GHz, 5GHz, and 6GHz bands.
fullExplanation: WiFi operates in unlicensed frequency bands. The 2.4GHz band has 14 channels (20MHz wide) but only 3 are non-overlapping (1, 6, 11). The 5GHz band has 24+ non-overlapping 20MHz channels and supports channel bonding (40/80/160MHz). The 6GHz band (WiFi 6E) adds 59 non-overlapping 20MHz channels with minimal interference. Wider channels = more throughput but more interference and shorter range.

bands.cons: Congested (microwaves, Bluetooth, baby monitors), only 3 usable channels

bands.pros: Less congested, more channels, supports 80/160MHz bonding
bands.cons: Shorter range, worse wall penetration, DFS channels require radar detection

bands.pros: Minimal interference, 320MHz channels in WiFi 7, very high throughput
bands.cons: Shortest range, requires WiFi 6E/7 devices, limited legacy device support

keyFacts: 2.4GHz channels 1, 6, 11 are the only non-overlapping ones — use these
DFS (Dynamic Frequency Selection): 5GHz channels must yield to radar
TPC (Transmit Power Control): reduce power to avoid interfering with radar
Channel bonding doubles/quadruples throughput but increases interference risk
WiFi 7 MLO (Multi-Link Operation): use multiple bands simultaneously
Regulatory domains: channel availability varies by country (FCC, ETSI, etc.)
